cards.back: A search algorithm that checks each element of a list, one by one, to find a specific item.
The algorithm starts at the first element and compares it to the target value, if a match is found, the search stops. If not, the algorithm moves to the next element and repeats the process.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of sequential searching?
cards.back: O(n), where n is the number of elements in the list, as the algorithm checks each element once.
Sequential searching is used when the data is not indexed or when the index is not available for faster search times.
No, sequential searching is inefficient as it checks each element one by one, making it slower for large datasets.
